content.thinking:
The conflict is clear:
- HEAD (PR branch) adds two new memory entries: "Dashboard Metrics Architecture" and "A-Guy memory system bootstrap smoke test"
- origin/dev deleted those same two lines (no corresponding additions)

Since this PR (`kody-memorize-20260512`) is specifically about memorizing memories, keeping the two new entries from HEAD is the right call. The conflict is asymmetric — dev deleted what HEAD added — and the intent of the PR is to add these memories, so I keep the HEAD side.

Let me resolve it by removing the conflict markers and keeping the two new entries.

content.text: The conflict is asymmetric: HEAD (the PR) adds two memory entries that `origin/dev` removed. Since this PR is specifically about memorizing (adding) these memories, I keep the HEAD side.

file.content:
# Kody memory index

One line per memory. The `memory-writer` job maintains this file — do not edit by hand.
To add a memory, drop a sticky note in [`inbox/`](inbox/README.md) and the job will file it.

Each entry: `- [Title](id.md) — one-line hook (type: <type>)`.
- [User prefers concise answers](user-prefers-concise-answers.md) — The user explicitly stated a preference for short, simple answers in responses. (type: user)
- [Kody does not address issues on goal branches directly](kody-does-not-address-issues-on-goal-branches-directly.md) — The Kody engine cannot directly address issues on goal branches; new work must be initiated from the default branch. (type: feedback)
- [Dashboard Metrics Architecture](dashboard-metrics.md) — User activity tracked via UserStats; createdAt acts as first-active proxy; metrics include time-window breakdowns for active users, guests, conversion, and returns. (type: architecture)
- [A-Guy memory system bootstrap smoke test](aguy-memory-bootstrap.md) — Verify A-Guy's memory-writer drains the inbox and files notes correctly. (type: lesson)
